milestoneTitle: Phase M9.19a.1 — Draft eyebrow reads source tool name. Phone review of M9.19a showed drafts rendered 'Draft · Draft' in the eyebrow because buildLearnItems set both kind and trackLabel to the literal 'Draft'. Drafts are authored from a specific Tools-card pin, so the source tool IS the effective track for that draft. buildLearnItems now looks up the source tool via sourceToolId and uses its name (e.g. 'Claude Code', 'Cursor') as the trackLabel; falls back to 'Draft' if the lookup fails. Eyebrow now reads 'Draft · Claude Code' instead of the tautological 'Draft · Draft'. Drafts remain the stress-test tile type for the unified .dash-tile layout — they carry the densest slot load (kind + track + summary + coverage pills + grab + pin + mastery + delete), so future slot additions should be validated against draft tiles first.
